page: Amazon Web Services z1d.metal is a bare-metal server instance featuring a high-frequency Intel Xeon 8151 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs. Operating at a sustained core frequency of 4.0 GHz, this x86_64 architecture platform delivers dedicated compute resources with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes 24 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX and AVX-512F, which assist in vector calculations and cryptographic operations.

The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. In memory benchmarks, the platform demonstrates strong performance, achieving a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 217.08 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 92.41 GB/sec. Local storage is bundled as an 1800 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance testing indicates average capabilities across standard compute workloads, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 12108.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 45079.06. Database operations on Redis reach 47.0 million SET operations per second. However, the hardware shows limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks, with text generation for a medium 7B LLM model registering at a weak 7.44 tokens per second.

Given the combination of high-frequency dedicated cores, substantial memory capacity, and fast local NVMe storage, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, electronic design automation, and high-performance computing tasks that require high single-core speeds without GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services z1d.metal is a bare-metal server configured with a high-frequency Intel Xeon 8151 processor, offering 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 4.0 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory alongside 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results show strong memory performance, with large block read bandwidth reaching 217.08 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 92.41 GB/sec. Compute benchmarks place the server in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 45079.06 and a Redis throughput of 47.0 million SET operations per second. While it exhibits weak performance in LLM text generation, its high clock speed and high memory-to-core ratio make it highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and workloads requiring rapid single-threaded execution.
og_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.metal is a bare-metal server featuring a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-frequency workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.metal is a bare-metal server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1800 GB NVMe storage, optimized for high-frequency compute tasks.
tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal platform combining dedicated 4.0 GHz processor cores with substantial DDR4 memory and local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8151 processor with 48 vCPUs operating at a high 4.0 GHz frequency
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing strong large-block read and write bandwidth
1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for consistent data transfer
Best suited for relational databases, EDA, and high-frequency single-threaded workloads
